\documentclass{article}

\usepackage{amsmath}
\usepackage{amsthm}

\newtheorem{lemma}{Lemma}

\begin{document}

\title{A Toy Averaging Lemma}
\author{Test Author}
\maketitle

\begin{abstract}
We record an elementary bound on the average of a function against a
probability density. The material is self-contained toy content.
\end{abstract}

\section{An Averaging Bound}
Let $f : [0, 1] \to \mathbb{R}$ and let $p : [0, 1] \to [0, \infty)$ satisfy
$\int_0^1 p(x)\, dx = 1$.

\begin{lemma}
\label{lem:average}
Suppose $|f(x)| \le M$ for all $x \in [0, 1]$. Then
\[
  \left| \int_0^1 f(x)\, p(x)\, dx \right| \le M.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
The integral $\int_0^1 f(x)\, p(x)\, dx$ exists, and by the triangle inequality
for integrals we have
\[
  \left| \int_0^1 f(x)\, p(x)\, dx \right|
    \le \int_0^1 |f(x)|\, p(x)\, dx
    \le M \int_0^1 p(x)\, dx = M,
\]
where the last step uses $\int_0^1 p(x)\, dx = 1$.
\end{proof}

\end{document}
